explanation:
**Environmental sustainability** is key in the 21st century. Composting is a concrete action you can take to reduce waste and emissions. By understanding your composting process, you're taking responsibility.

## How It's Calculated

This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to values outside reasonable parameters.

## Final Notes

This calculation is for reference only. For critical decisions about garden management or soil amendments, consult with a specialized professional. Values are updated for 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: How do I know when my compost is finished?
faq.a: It should be dark brown in color, smell like soil, and have no recognizable materials remaining.

faq.q: What's the right moisture level for compost?
faq.a: Aim for 50-60% moisture (like a wrung-out sponge). Too dry slows down decomposition.
30:1 is ideal. Greens (nitrogen): leaves, coffee grounds. Browns (carbon): paper, dry leaves.

faq.q: Why does my compost smell bad?
faq.a: Usually too much moisture or not enough nitrogen. Add dry material or turn more frequently.

faq.q: How small should compost materials be?
faq.a: Chopping materials into pieces under 2 inches speeds up the process 2-3 times faster.
Yes, all our calculators are free and don't require registration.
Results are estimates using internationally validated standard formulas. For critical decisions, consult a professional.

faq.q: Do you store information about my calculations?
faq.a: No. Calculations happen entirely in your browser. We don't send any data to our servers.
